ID:       ddx-b790449b
Title:    ddx work should auto-route execution without project config
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   ddx, area:routing, area:cli, kind:enhancement, priority:high
Desc:     Operators should not need to author any project-level routing config (.ddx/config.yaml, --harness, --profile, --model, --provider) for `ddx work` to drain a project's bead queue. The platform should auto-select harness, model, and provider based on bead complexity, retry/fall back on failures, and decompose work that is too coarse for one dispatch.

## Observed behavior (HELIX project, 2026-05-01)

Running `ddx work --once --local` from `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`:

```
warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts
▶ helix-13cfe23f: Align HELIX execution contract to shipped DDx execute-loop / execute-bead capabilities
✓ helix-13cfe23f → error: routing under-specified: pass at least one of Harness/Profile/ModelRef/Model/Provider, or auto-selection inputs
project: /Users/erik/Projects/helix
completed: 1  |  successes: 0  |  failures: 1
```

Workarounds tried, all failed or were rejected by users as the wrong layer to address this:

1. `ddx work --once --local --profile cheap` — same routing-under-specified error.
2. `ddx work --once --local --harness openrouter` — `harness "openrouter": no configured provider matches type "openrouter" (available: [])`, despite `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` having multiple openrouter-typed providers.
3. `--min-tier cheap` — unknown flag.

By contrast, the same `ddx work` invocation succeeds for the DDx project itself — workers in `ddx server workers list` show recent successful runs against ddx-* beads. So the per-project config gap is the difference, not anything global.

## Expected behavior

`ddx work` (and `ddx agent execute-loop` directly) should:

1. Auto-select routing for each bead with no flags and no project config required, given that the global agent config (`~/.config/agent/config.yaml`) has providers configured.
2. Make routing decisions from bead complexity: doc-only or single-file changes → cheap tier; multi-file refactors → standard; cross-cutting work → smart. The 'triage complexity gate' warning suggests this exists but is disabled by default.
3. Retry on transient failure within the same tier; escalate to a higher tier on substantive failure (this is documented behavior — `winning_tier=exhausted` events show the escalation chain works once dispatched, but auto-routing has to dispatch first).
4. When a bead is too coarse for any tier to execute reliably, either decompose it into child beads or surface a 'needs operator triage' status with a clear reason — never silently wedge with `routing under-specified`.
5. Default to using the global agent config's `default_provider` when no project override exists, instead of treating the absence of project config as a hard error.

## Why this matters

- **Adoption friction**: Every new project that adopts DDx hits this wall. Authoring `.ddx/config.yaml` requires understanding DDx's routing internals before an operator can run `ddx work` once.
- **Methodology coupling**: HELIX's value proposition includes 'supervised autopilot' — the autopilot is supposed to drain ready beads. When `ddx work` requires per-project routing config, HELIX projects have to ship operational setup that distracts from the methodology.
- **Operator surprise**: The DDx-vs-HELIX boundary contract (HELIX/CONTRACT-001) explicitly says HELIX should not own routing config. But projects can't run `ddx work` without it. That's a contract violation manifesting as setup burden.
- **Triage gate disabled by default**: The warning 'triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts' is emitted on every run but the operator has no obvious way to enable it. The default should be enabled with a documented opt-out, not the inverse.

## Suggested approach (not prescriptive)

1. Audit the auto-selection input requirements: what inputs are needed to pick a tier when no project config is present? Likely the bead's labels, `kind`, file-touch estimate, and prior-attempt history. All available without operator input.
2. Make 'use global agent config defaults' the fallback when project routing is unspecified — currently the code requires explicit project routing OR explicit flags.
3. Enable triage complexity gate by default; document the opt-out flag.
4. Add an integration test: `ddx work --once --local` succeeds against a fresh project with no `.ddx/config.yaml` and a single bead in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, given a minimal `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` with one provider.

## Out of scope

- Changing the global agent config schema. The fix is to reduce dependency on per-project config, not redesign the global one.
- Per-bead model overrides for advanced users — those should remain available via flags but should not be required for the common case.

## Reference

- HELIX alignment review where this surfaced: `docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-05-01-public-examples.md` (in the easel/helix repo). The audit's 12 follow-up beads were drained via direct sub-agent dispatch instead of `ddx work` because of this gap.
Accept:   1. `ddx work --once --local` succeeds (exits 0 with at least one successful bead close OR a clean 'no ready work' exit) on a project that has only `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and no `.ddx/config.yaml`, when the global agent config has at least one provider configured.

2. `ddx work` does not emit `routing under-specified` for any bead unless the global agent config is itself empty — that case must produce a clear error pointing at the global config, not a per-project routing flag list.

3. Triage complexity gate is enabled by default. The warning 'triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts' must not appear in the default invocation. Opt-out flag exists and is documented.

4. Auto-tier selection picks a tier from bead metadata alone (labels, kind, estimated scope) for beads that have those fields, with the global default_provider for routing when no per-project config is present.

5. Coarse-bead handling: when no tier can confidently execute a bead (e.g. it spans more than N files or N hours of estimated work), DDx either decomposes the bead (records this as decomposition events on the bead) or emits a `needs_triage` status (not `execution_failed`) with a clear reason.

6. Failure recovery: transient failures (network, rate limit, timeout) retry within the selected tier; substantive failures (probe_ok+exec_fail or harness errors) escalate to the next tier per existing escalation logic.

7. Integration test in the DDx test suite: `tests/integration/zero-config-work_test.go` (or equivalent) creates a temp project with one bead and a minimal global config, runs `ddx work --once --local`, asserts the bead closes successfully with no project config files created.

8. Documentation update: `docs/getting-started.md` (or equivalent) shows the zero-config flow as the default, with project config presented as an advanced override rather than a prerequisite.

Find the retry/escalation logic in this DDx repo. I'm working on a bead that needs to add an integration test verifying that:
1. transient failures retry within the same tier (cheap)
2. substantive failures escalate to next tier (cheap → standard → smart)
3. The starting profile is now "profile=cheap" by default (set by recent commit 3ddf3d52 "metadata-driven tier inference replaces always-cheap default")

I need to know:
- Where is the retry-within-tier logic? File paths and function names.
- Where is the escalation-between-tiers logic? File paths and function names. Look for things like "winning_tier", "exhausted", "escalate", "next tier".
- Where is the auto-route default that was changed in commit 3ddf3d52? Look at that commit.
- Where do existing routing/execute-loop tests live? Are there integration tests in cli/cmd/ or tests/integration/ ?
- What's the difference between "transient" failure (network, rate limit, timeout) and "substantive" failure (probe_ok+exec_fail) in code?
- Is there a test harness/mock that simulates failure responses from harnesses?

Report concise findings with file:line references. Under 400 words.

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":             true,
22		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
23		"land_conflict":                true,
24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
25	}
26	
27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
32	}
33	
34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
36	// locking out a provider for too long.
37	//
38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
42	
43	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
44	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
46		if probeResult != "" {
47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
48		}
49		if detail != "" {
50			body += "\n" + detail
51		}
52		return body
53	}
54	
55	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
56	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
57	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
58	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
59	
60	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
61	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
62	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
63	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
64	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
65		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
66		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
67		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
68		Status     string  `json:"status"`
69		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
70		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
71	}
72	
73	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
74	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
75	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
76	type EscalationSummary struct {
77		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
78		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
79		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
80		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
81	}
82	
83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
88	// SuccessStatus.
89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
90		out := EscalationSummary{
91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
93		}
94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
96		}
97		for _, a := range attempts {
98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
101			}
102		}
103		return out
104	}
105	
106	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
107	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
108	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
109	type BeadEventAppender interface {
110		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
111	}
112	
113	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
114	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
115	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
116	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
117	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
118	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
119		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
120			return nil
121		}
122		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
123		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
124		if err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
128			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
129			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
130			Body:      string(body),
131			Actor:     actor,
132			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
133			CreatedAt: createdAt,
134		})
135	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5	
6		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
7	)
8	
9	// Retry policy logical outcome names. These are the policy's semantic
10	// vocabulary, distinct from execution status and failure-mode constants.
11	const (
12		// RetryOutcomeSuccess marks a successful merge; no retry needed.
13		RetryOutcomeSuccess = "success"
14	
15		// RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt marks an attempt where the agent
16		// produced no commits. Retryable with a higher MinPower.
17		RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt = "no_changes_after_attempt"
18	
19		// RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability marks an attempt where the
20		// post-merge reviewer returned BLOCK or REQUEST_CHANGES, indicating the
21		// model's output did not meet the acceptance criteria. Retryable with a
22		// higher MinPower.
23		RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability = "review_blocked_capability"
24	
25		// RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted marks an attempt where the passthrough
26		// envelope conflicts with the power bounds (blocked_by_passthrough_constraint
27		// or agent_power_unsatisfied). Power escalation cannot resolve this;
28		// the policy stops instead of mutating the passthrough.
29		RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted = "passthrough_exhausted"
30	
31		// RetryOutcomeSetupFailure marks a deterministic infrastructure failure that
32		// power escalation cannot fix: harness not installed, no viable provider,
33		// auth error, etc. The policy stops.
34		RetryOutcomeSetupFailure = "deterministic_setup_failure"
35	
36		// RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress marks an attempt where MinPower cannot be
37		// raised any further — either because the next threshold would exceed
38		// MaxPower, or because the catalog has no higher power tier available.
39		// The policy stops.
40		RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress = "max_power_no_progress"
41	
42		// RetryOutcomeOther marks any outcome not eligible for power escalation.
43		RetryOutcomeOther = "other"
44	)
45	
46	// RetryDecision is the policy output for one bead attempt outcome.
47	// It never includes DDx-selected harness, provider, model, or model-ref pins;
48	// only a MinPower adjustment is returned.
49	type RetryDecision struct {
50		// ShouldRetry indicates whether the bead should be retried with escalated
51		// MinPower. false means the policy decided to stop.
52		ShouldRetry bool
53	
54		// NextMinPower is the MinPower to use for the next attempt.
55		// Only meaningful when ShouldRetry is true.
56		NextMinPower int
57	
58		// Reason classifies why the policy decided to retry or stop.
59		// Populated for both outcomes and used in audit records.
60		Reason string
61	
62		// Evidence provides additional context for the audit record.
63		Evidence string
64	}
65	
66	// ClassifyRetryOutcome maps an attempt's status and failure_mode into a
67	// logical retry outcome that EvaluateRetryPolicy uses to make its decision.
68	//
69	// Passthrough exhaustion (blocked_by_passthrough_constraint or
70	// agent_power_unsatisfied) is detected first and always results in a stop,
71	// regardless of the status string. Deterministic setup failures (harness not
72	// installed, no viable provider, auth error) are detected next. All other
73	// classification is done from status alone.
74	func ClassifyRetryOutcome(status, failureMode string) string {
75		// Passthrough exhaustion stops regardless of other signals; inspecting
76		// the failure mode is sufficient and does not require reading string values
77		// from the passthrough envelope itself.
78		switch failureMode {
79		case FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint, FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied:
80			return RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted
81		}
82	
83		// Deterministic setup failures that power escalation cannot fix.
84		switch failureMode {
85		case FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled, FailureModeNoViableProvider, FailureModeAuthError:
86			return RetryOutcomeSetupFailure
87		}
88	
89		switch status {
90		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
91			return RetryOutcomeSuccess
92		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
93			return RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt
94		case ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges,
95			ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction:
96			return RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability
97		}
98	
99		return RetryOutcomeOther
100	}
101	
102	// EvaluateRetryPolicy makes the retry/stop decision for one bead attempt.
103	//
104	// outcome must come from ClassifyRetryOutcome. actualPower is the power level
105	// used in the prior attempt (from routing evidence's actual_power field).
106	// currentMinPower and maxPower are the bounds currently in effect for the bead.
107	// models is the agent model catalog; when non-empty, top-1 (the strongest tier)
108	// is used as the escalation target.
109	//
110	// The returned RetryDecision never includes DDx-selected harness, provider,
111	// model, or model-ref pins — only a new MinPower bound. The passthrough
112	// envelope is neither read nor mutated.
113	func EvaluateRetryPolicy(
114		outcome string,
115		actualPower int,
116		currentMinPower int,
117		maxPower int,
118		models []agentlib.ModelInfo,
119	) RetryDecision {
120		switch outcome {
121		case RetryOutcomeSuccess:
122			return RetryDecision{
123				ShouldRetry: false,
124				Reason:      RetryOutcomeSuccess,
125				Evidence:    "attempt succeeded; no retry needed",
126			}
127	
128		case RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted:
129			return RetryDecision{
130				ShouldRetry: false,
131				Reason:      RetryOutcomePassthroughExhausted,
132				Evidence:    "passthrough constraint or agent power bounds incompatible; retry would not resolve",
133			}
134	
135		case RetryOutcomeSetupFailure:
136			return RetryDecision{
137				ShouldRetry: false,
138				Reason:      RetryOutcomeSetupFailure,
139				Evidence:    "deterministic setup failure; power escalation cannot fix",
140			}
141	
142		case RetryOutcomeNoChangesAfterAttempt, RetryOutcomeReviewBlockedCapability:
143			nextMinPower := computeNextMinPower(models, actualPower, currentMinPower)
144	
145			// MaxPower must be preserved across retries. If escalation would
146			// produce a NextMinPower that violates the MaxPower bound, stop.
147			if maxPower > 0 && nextMinPower > maxPower {
148				return RetryDecision{
149					ShouldRetry: false,
150					Reason:      RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress,
151					Evidence: fmt.Sprintf(
152						"next_min_power=%d would exceed max_power=%d; cannot escalate within bounds",
153						nextMinPower, maxPower),
154				}
155			}
156	
157			// If the computed next threshold is not higher than the power level
158			// the prior attempt already used, escalating to it would not constrain
159			// routing to a stronger model — the catalog has no higher tier.
160			if actualPower > 0 && nextMinPower <= actualPower {
161				return RetryDecision{
162					ShouldRetry: false,
163					Reason:      RetryOutcomeMaxPowerNoProgress,
164					Evidence: fmt.Sprintf(
165						"actual_power=%d; no higher power tier available in catalog",
166						actualPower),
167				}
168			}
169	
170			return RetryDecision{
171				ShouldRetry:  true,
172				NextMinPower: nextMinPower,
173				Reason:       outcome,
174				Evidence: fmt.Sprintf(
175					"actual_power=%d next_min_power=%d",
176					actualPower, nextMinPower),
177			}
178	
179		default:
180			return RetryDecision{
181				ShouldRetry: false,
182				Reason:      RetryOutcomeOther,
183				Evidence:    "outcome not eligible for power escalation: " + outcome,
184			}
185		}
186	}
187	
188	// computeNextMinPower returns the MinPower for the next escalated attempt.
189	// When the models catalog is non-empty, returns TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)
190	// — the minimum power required to reach only the strongest model tier.
191	// When the catalog is empty, bumps above the highest observed power by a
192	// fixed delta.
193	func computeNextMinPower(models []agentlib.ModelInfo, actualPower, currentMinPower int) int {
194		top1 := TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)
195		if top1 > 0 {
196			return top1
197		}
198		// No catalog: heuristic bump above the highest observed power.
199		base := currentMinPower
200		if actualPower > base {
201			base = actualPower
202		}
203		if base == 0 {
204			return 50
205		}
206		return base + 10
207	}

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.

Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
stay decoupled from harness installation details.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities agent
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log`,
		PersistentPreRunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {

2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"sync"
7	)
8	
9	// InfrastructureFailurePatterns are detail substrings that indicate a
10	// transient infrastructure-level failure (provider 502, network unreachable,
11	// command not found, auth/quota exhausted) rather than a model-capability
12	// failure (test failed, build failed, no changes after attempt).
13	//
14	// Infrastructure failures should not consume escalation budget — the bead
15	// should be deferred with a retry-after, not retried at the next tier with
16	// a more expensive model. The model wasn't given a fair chance.
17	//
18	// Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the failure
19	// detail string. Any new pattern added here is automatically picked up by
20	// IsInfrastructureFailure callers.
21	var InfrastructureFailurePatterns = []string{
22		// HTTP-level provider unavailability
23		"502", "503", "504",
24		"bad gateway",
25		"service unavailable",
26		"gateway timeout",
27		// Network-level unreachability
28		"connection refused",
29		"no such host",
30		"no route to host",
31		"network is unreachable",
32		"i/o timeout",
33		"context deadline exceeded",
34		// Auth/quota exhaustion (operator-fixable, not a model fault)
35		"401", "429",
36		"unauthorized",
37		"rate limit",
38		"ratelimit",
39		"quota exceeded",
40		"insufficient quota",
41		"insufficient_quota",
42		// Subprocess harness binary missing or unstartable
43		"command not found",
44		"executable file not found",
45		"no such file or directory",
46	}
47	
48	// IsInfrastructureFailure reports whether the given failure status + detail
49	// indicates a transient infrastructure problem the model could not have
50	// fixed. Only execution_failed can be infrastructure; other escalatable
51	// statuses are semantic outcomes that should proceed through the tier ladder.
52	// Returns false for statuses whose detail does not match any known
53	// infrastructure pattern.
54	//
55	// Used by the execute-loop to decide whether to (a) defer the bead with a
56	// retry-after and try the same tier later, or (b) burn through to the next
57	// tier as the standard escalation policy.
58	func IsInfrastructureFailure(status, detail string) bool {
59		if status != "execution_failed" {
60			return false
61		}
62		if detail == "" {
63			return false
64		}
65		lower := strings.ToLower(detail)
66		for _, p := range InfrastructureFailurePatterns {
67			if strings.Contains(lower, p) {
68				return true
69			}
70		}
71		return false
72	}
73	
74	// DefaultMaxCostUSD is the per-execute-loop dollar cap on accumulated billed
75	// spend. Exceeding the cap halts further bead claiming so an execute-loop
76	// run cannot silently burn through arbitrary openrouter / pay-per-token
77	// credits. Local LLMs and subscription-bundled providers do not count
78	// toward the cap (see CountsTowardCostCap).
79	//
80	// $100 is large enough not to interrupt normal subscription-driven work
81	// (where billed cost stays at $0) and small enough to surface a clear stop
82	// signal before runaway openrouter usage.
83	const DefaultMaxCostUSD = 100.0
84	
85	// CostClassBilled is the HarnessInfo.CostClass value the agent uses for
86	// pay-per-token providers. Free local providers report "free" and
87	// subscription-bundled providers report "subscription".
88	const CostClassBilled = "expensive"
89	
90	// CountsTowardCostCap returns true when an attempt's reported CostUSD
91	// should accumulate against the loop's MaxCostUSD. Callers pass the
92	// HarnessInfo of the harness that ran the attempt; when info is nil the
93	// safe default is to count (treat unknown as billed).
94	func CountsTowardCostCap(isLocal, isSubscription bool, costClass string) bool {
95		if isLocal {
96			return false
97		}
98		if isSubscription {
99			return false
100		}
101		// CostClass "free" or "subscription" never bill; only "expensive" does.
102		if costClass != "" && costClass != CostClassBilled {
103			return false
104		}
105		return true
106	}
107	
108	// HarnessBilledLookup reports whether a harness's reported CostUSD should
109	// be accumulated against a CostCapTracker. Implementations typically
110	// consult a service.ListHarnesses snapshot. A nil lookup is treated as
111	// "count by default" (the safe option for unknown harnesses).
112	type HarnessBilledLookup func(harnessName string) bool
113	
114	// CostCapTracker accumulates billed cost for a single execute-loop run
115	// and reports when accumulated spend has reached the configured cap.
116	// Safe for concurrent use across goroutines (workers may call Add and
117	// Tripped concurrently).
118	//
119	// MaxUSD == 0 disables the cap; Tripped always returns (_, false). The
120	// Lookup callback decides which harnesses contribute to the running
121	// total — local and subscription-bundled providers should be excluded
122	// (see CountsTowardCostCap).
123	type CostCapTracker struct {
124		MaxUSD float64
125		Lookup HarnessBilledLookup
126	
127		mu    sync.Mutex
128		spent float64
129		// cache memoizes Lookup results so we do not rebuild a service for
130		// every reported attempt. Protected by mu.
131		cache map[string]bool
132	}
133	
134	// NewCostCapTracker constructs a tracker with the given dollar cap and
135	// lookup. Pass maxUSD <= 0 to disable the cap. A nil lookup is treated
136	// as "count by default" — every harness contributes.
137	func NewCostCapTracker(maxUSD float64, lookup HarnessBilledLookup) *CostCapTracker {
138		return &CostCapTracker{
139			MaxUSD: maxUSD,
140			Lookup: lookup,
141			cache:  map[string]bool{},
142		}
143	}
144	
145	// counts reports whether harnessName's CostUSD should accumulate. The
146	// result is memoized; callers that want fresh resolution should
147	// construct a new tracker. Lookups for empty harness names default to
148	// true (count) so accidentally-missing harness metadata never silently
149	// bypasses the cap.
150	func (t *CostCapTracker) counts(harnessName string) bool {
151		if harnessName == "" {
152			return true
153		}
154		t.mu.Lock()
155		if v, ok := t.cache[harnessName]; ok {
156			t.mu.Unlock()
157			return v
158		}
159		t.mu.Unlock()
160		result := true
161		if t.Lookup != nil {
162			result = t.Lookup(harnessName)
163		}
164		t.mu.Lock()
165		t.cache[harnessName] = result
166		t.mu.Unlock()
167		return result
168	}
169	
170	// Add accumulates costUSD against the running total when harnessName's
171	// billing class counts toward the cap. Non-positive cost is ignored.
172	func (t *CostCapTracker) Add(harnessName string, costUSD float64) {
173		if costUSD <= 0 {
174			return
175		}
176		if !t.counts(harnessName) {
177			return
178		}
179		t.mu.Lock()
180		t.spent += costUSD
181		t.mu.Unlock()
182	}
183	
184	// Spent returns the current accumulated billed total.
185	func (t *CostCapTracker) Spent() float64 {
186		t.mu.Lock()
187		defer t.mu.Unlock()
188		return t.spent
189	}
190	
191	// Tripped reports whether the accumulated spend has reached MaxUSD. When
192	// MaxUSD <= 0 the cap is disabled and Tripped returns (_, false).
193	// Returns the formatted operator-facing detail string when tripped so
194	// callers can populate a stop-the-loop ExecuteBeadReport.
195	func (t *CostCapTracker) Tripped() (string, bool) {
196		if t.MaxUSD <= 0 {
197			return "", false
198		}
199		t.mu.Lock()
200		spent := t.spent
201		t.mu.Unlock()
202		if spent < t.MaxUSD {
203			return "", false
204		}
205		return fmt.Sprintf("cost cap reached: $%.2f billed >= $%.2f cap; raise the cap or set 0 to disable. Subscription and local providers do not count.", spent, t.MaxUSD), true
206	}

12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)
14	
15	// TestEscalationTrailCheapFailStandardSucceed simulates the scenario from the
16	// bead description: the cheap provider returns an execution failure (analogous
17	// to a 502 from the inference host), the executor escalates to standard, which
18	// succeeds, and the bead is closed. The escalation trail is visible in bead events.
19	//
20	// Acceptance criterion: "Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard
21	// succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible"
22	func TestEscalationTrailCheapFailStandardSucceed(t *testing.T) {
23		store, targetBead, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
24	
25		// Track which tiers the executor was invoked with.
26		var attemptTiers []string
27	
28		// The executor simulates tier-based escalation by reading the tier from
29		// the report it is asked to build. In the real code path the tier is
30		// resolved by the agent service; here we inject it directly via
31		// ExecuteBeadReport.Tier.
32		//
33		// cheap  → execution_failed (provider down)
34		// standard → success
35		callCount := 0
36		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
37			Store: store,
38			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
39				callCount++
40				tier := escalation.TierCheap
41				if callCount > 1 {
42					tier = escalation.TierStandard
43				}
44				attemptTiers = append(attemptTiers, string(tier))
45	
46				// Append a tier-attempt event the same way the real escalating
47				// executor does, so the trail is visible in bead events.
48				_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
49					Kind:      "tier-attempt",
50					Actor:     "test",
51					Source:    "test",
52					CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
53				})
54	
55				if tier == escalation.TierCheap {
56					return ExecuteBeadReport{
57						BeadID:      beadID,
58						Tier:        string(tier),
59						Harness:     "mock-cheap",
60						Model:       "cheap-model",
61						Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
62						Detail:      "provider 502: connection refused",
63						ProbeResult: "error: 502",
64					}, nil
65				}
66				// Standard tier succeeds.
67				return ExecuteBeadReport{
68					BeadID:      beadID,
69					Tier:        string(tier),
70					Harness:     "mock-standard",
71					Model:       "standard-model",
72					Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
73					Detail:      "merged cleanly",
74					SessionID:   "sess-escalation",
75					ResultRev:   "abc12345",
76					ProbeResult: "ok",
77				}, nil
78			}),
79		}
80	
81		// Run the loop twice: first call returns cheap failure, second returns
82		// standard success. The Executor decides internally which tier to use
83		// based on callCount, mirroring the real escalation loop.
84		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "test-worker"}
85		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
86		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
87			Once: true,
88		})
89		require.NoError(t, err)
90	
91		// After a cheap failure the loop sees execution_failed and unclaims.
92		// On the next iteration (same Run call, same queue), the bead is still
93		// open and ready, so the executor is called again with the next tier.
94		//
95		// NOTE: because the store has a second bead, after the cheap failure the
96		// loop will attempt the second bead. To isolate this test to a single
97		// bead, we confirm the behaviour by checking the final bead state.
98		//
99		// The full escalation-within-a-single-claim behaviour is exercised by the
100		// CLI path (singleTierAttempt loop in runAgentExecuteLoop). This test
101		// validates that:
102		//  1. The executor is called per bead claim.
103		//  2. tier-attempt events are recorded.
104		//  3. When the second call succeeds, the bead is closed.
105		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, result.Attempts, 1)
106	
107		// The bead that succeeded must be closed.
108		events, err := store.Events(targetBead.ID)
109		require.NoError(t, err)
110	
111		// At minimum a "tier-attempt" event must have been appended.
112		var tierAttemptFound bool
113		for _, ev := range events {
114			if ev.Kind == "tier-attempt" {
115				tierAttemptFound = true
116				break
117			}
118		}
119		assert.True(t, tierAttemptFound, "tier-attempt event must appear in bead events for escalation trail")
120	}
121	
122	// TestEscalationTierRecordedInFinalEvent verifies that when a bead report
123	// carries Tier and ProbeResult, those values appear in the loop event body.
124	func TestEscalationTierRecordedInFinalEvent(t *testing.T) {
125		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
126	
127		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
128			Store: store,
129			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
130				return ExecuteBeadReport{
131					BeadID:      beadID,
132					Tier:        "standard",
133					ProbeResult: "ok (2 candidates)",
134					Harness:     "claude",
135					Model:       "claude-sonnet-4-6",
136					Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
137					Detail:      "merged",
138					SessionID:   "sess-tier",
139					ResultRev:   "def45678",
140				}, nil
141			}),
142		}
143	
144		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "test-worker"}
145		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
146		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
147			Once: true,
148		})
149		require.NoError(t, err)
150	
151		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
152		require.NoError(t, err)
153		require.NotEmpty(t, events)
154	
155		// Find the execute-bead event (the loop-level event).
156		var loopEvent *bead.BeadEvent
157		for i := range events {
158			if events[i].Kind == "execute-bead" {
159				loopEvent = &events[i]
160				break
161			}
162		}
163		require.NotNil(t, loopEvent, "execute-bead event must be present")
164		assert.Contains(t, loopEvent.Body, "tier=standard", "tier must appear in loop event body")
165		assert.Contains(t, loopEvent.Body, "probe_result=ok (2 candidates)", "probe_result must appear in loop event body")
166	}

30	// recordingAppender captures events appended by AppendEscalationSummaryEvent

32	type recordingAppender struct {
33		events []struct {

35			event bead.BeadEvent
36		}
37	}
38	
39	func (r *recordingAppender) AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error {
40		r.events = append(r.events, struct {
41			id    string
42			event bead.BeadEvent
43		}{id: id, event: event})
44		return nil
45	}

47	// TestEscalationSummary exercises the 3-tier escalation scenario from the
48	// bead: cheap fails, standard fails, smart succeeds. The emitted
49	// kind:escalation-summary event body must contain all three tier records
50	// with correct statuses, costs, and a winning_tier/wasted_cost_usd roll-up.
51	func TestEscalationSummary(t *testing.T) {
52		attempts := []TierAttemptRecord{
53			{Tier: "cheap", Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: statusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.02, DurationMS: 1200},
54			{Tier: "standard", Harness: "codex", Model: "standard-model", Status: statusNoChanges, CostUSD: 0.15, DurationMS: 3400},
55			{Tier: "smart", Harness: "claude", Model: "smart-model", Status: statusSuccess, CostUSD: 0.80, DurationMS: 9000},
56		}
57	
58		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, "smart")
59		require.Equal(t, "smart", summary.WinningTier)
60		require.Len(t, summary.TiersAttempted, 3)
61		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, summary.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
62		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, summary.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9, "cheap (0.02) + standard (0.15) wasted, smart succeeded")
63		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", summary.TiersAttempted[0].Tier)
64		assert.Equal(t, statusExecutionFailed, summary.TiersAttempted[0].Status)
65		assert.Equal(t, statusNoChanges, summary.TiersAttempted[1].Status)
66		assert.Equal(t, statusSuccess, summary.TiersAttempted[2].Status)
67	
68		// Now wire through AppendEscalationSummaryEvent and verify the emitted
69		// event has kind:escalation-summary, a summary line, and a JSON body
70		// that round-trips to the same EscalationSummary.
71		appender := &recordingAppender{}
72		err := AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender, "ddx-test-1", "test-actor", attempts, "smart", time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
73		require.NoError(t, err)
74		require.Len(t, appender.events, 1)
75	
76		ev := appender.events[0]
77		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-test-1", ev.id)
78		assert.Equal(t, "escalation-summary", ev.event.Kind)
79		assert.Equal(t, "test-actor", ev.event.Actor)
80		assert.Contains(t, ev.event.Summary, "winning_tier=smart")

23	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
24	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
25	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
26		parent := os.Environ()
27		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
28		for _, kv := range parent {
29			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
30				continue
31			}
32			env = append(env, kv)
33		}
34		return env
35	}

37	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
38	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
39	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
40		t.Helper()
41		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
42		cmd.Dir = dir
43		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
44		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
45		if err != nil {
46			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
47		}
48		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
49	}

2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":             true,
22		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
23		"land_conflict":                true,
24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
25	}
26	
27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
32	}
33	
34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
36	// locking out a provider for too long.
37	//
38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
42	
43	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
44	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
46		if probeResult != "" {
47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
48		}
49		if detail != "" {
50			body += "\n" + detail
51		}
52		return body
53	}
54	
55	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
56	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
57	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
58	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
59	
60	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
61	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
62	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
63	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
64	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
65		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
66		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
67		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
68		Status     string  `json:"status"`
69		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
70		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
71	}
72	
73	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
74	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
75	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
76	type EscalationSummary struct {
77		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
78		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
79		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
80		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
81	}
82	
83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
88	// SuccessStatus.
89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
90		out := EscalationSummary{
91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
93		}
94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
96		}
97		for _, a := range attempts {
98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
101			}
102		}
103		return out
104	}
105	
106	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
107	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
108	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
109	type BeadEventAppender interface {
110		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
111	}
112	
113	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
114	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
115	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
116	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
117	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
118	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
119		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
120			return nil
121		}
122		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
123		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
124		if err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
128			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
129			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
130			Body:      string(body),
131			Actor:     actor,
132			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
133			CreatedAt: createdAt,
134		})
135	}

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"sync"
7	)
8	
9	// InfrastructureFailurePatterns are detail substrings that indicate a
10	// transient infrastructure-level failure (provider 502, network unreachable,
11	// command not found, auth/quota exhausted) rather than a model-capability
12	// failure (test failed, build failed, no changes after attempt).
13	//
14	// Infrastructure failures should not consume escalation budget — the bead
15	// should be deferred with a retry-after, not retried at the next tier with
16	// a more expensive model. The model wasn't given a fair chance.
17	//
18	// Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the failure
19	// detail string. Any new pattern added here is automatically picked up by
20	// IsInfrastructureFailure callers.
21	var InfrastructureFailurePatterns = []string{
22		// HTTP-level provider unavailability
23		"502", "503", "504",
24		"bad gateway",
25		"service unavailable",
26		"gateway timeout",
27		// Network-level unreachability
28		"connection refused",
29		"no such host",
30		"no route to host",
31		"network is unreachable",
32		"i/o timeout",
33		"context deadline exceeded",
34		// Auth/quota exhaustion (operator-fixable, not a model fault)
35		"401", "429",
36		"unauthorized",
37		"rate limit",
38		"ratelimit",
39		"quota exceeded",
40		"insufficient quota",
41		"insufficient_quota",
42		// Subprocess harness binary missing or unstartable
43		"command not found",
44		"executable file not found",
45		"no such file or directory",
46	}
47	
48	// IsInfrastructureFailure reports whether the given failure status + detail
49	// indicates a transient infrastructure problem the model could not have
50	// fixed. Only execution_failed can be infrastructure; other escalatable
51	// statuses are semantic outcomes that should proceed through the tier ladder.
52	// Returns false for statuses whose detail does not match any known
53	// infrastructure pattern.
54	//
55	// Used by the execute-loop to decide whether to (a) defer the bead with a
56	// retry-after and try the same tier later, or (b) burn through to the next
57	// tier as the standard escalation policy.
58	func IsInfrastructureFailure(status, detail string) bool {
59		if status != "execution_failed" {
60			return false
61		}
62		if detail == "" {
63			return false
64		}
65		lower := strings.ToLower(detail)
66		for _, p := range InfrastructureFailurePatterns {
67			if strings.Contains(lower, p) {
68				return true
69			}
70		}
71		return false
72	}
73	
74	// DefaultMaxCostUSD is the per-execute-loop dollar cap on accumulated billed
75	// spend. Exceeding the cap halts further bead claiming so an execute-loop
76	// run cannot silently burn through arbitrary openrouter / pay-per-token
77	// credits. Local LLMs and subscription-bundled providers do not count
78	// toward the cap (see CountsTowardCostCap).
79	//
80	// $100 is large enough not to interrupt normal subscription-driven work
81	// (where billed cost stays at $0) and small enough to surface a clear stop
82	// signal before runaway openrouter usage.
83	const DefaultMaxCostUSD = 100.0
84	
85	// CostClassBilled is the HarnessInfo.CostClass value the agent uses for
86	// pay-per-token providers. Free local providers report "free" and
87	// subscription-bundled providers report "subscription".
88	const CostClassBilled = "expensive"
89	
90	// CountsTowardCostCap returns true when an attempt's reported CostUSD
91	// should accumulate against the loop's MaxCostUSD. Callers pass the
92	// HarnessInfo of the harness that ran the attempt; when info is nil the
93	// safe default is to count (treat unknown as billed).
94	func CountsTowardCostCap(isLocal, isSubscription bool, costClass string) bool {
95		if isLocal {
96			return false
97		}
98		if isSubscription {
99			return false
100		}
101		// CostClass "free" or "subscription" never bill; only "expensive" does.
102		if costClass != "" && costClass != CostClassBilled {
103			return false
104		}
105		return true
106	}
107	
108	// HarnessBilledLookup reports whether a harness's reported CostUSD should
109	// be accumulated against a CostCapTracker. Implementations typically
110	// consult a service.ListHarnesses snapshot. A nil lookup is treated as
111	// "count by default" (the safe option for unknown harnesses).
112	type HarnessBilledLookup func(harnessName string) bool
113	
114	// CostCapTracker accumulates billed cost for a single execute-loop run
115	// and reports when accumulated spend has reached the configured cap.
116	// Safe for concurrent use across goroutines (workers may call Add and
117	// Tripped concurrently).
118	//
119	// MaxUSD == 0 disables the cap; Tripped always returns (_, false). The
120	// Lookup callback decides which harnesses contribute to the running
121	// total — local and subscription-bundled providers should be excluded
122	// (see CountsTowardCostCap).
123	type CostCapTracker struct {
124		MaxUSD float64
125		Lookup HarnessBilledLookup
126	
127		mu    sync.Mutex
128		spent float64
129		// cache memoizes Lookup results so we do not rebuild a service for
130		// every reported attempt. Protected by mu.
131		cache map[string]bool
132	}
133	
134	// NewCostCapTracker constructs a tracker with the given dollar cap and
135	// lookup. Pass maxUSD <= 0 to disable the cap. A nil lookup is treated
136	// as "count by default" — every harness contributes.
137	func NewCostCapTracker(maxUSD float64, lookup HarnessBilledLookup) *CostCapTracker {
138		return &CostCapTracker{
139			MaxUSD: maxUSD,
140			Lookup: lookup,
141			cache:  map[string]bool{},
142		}
143	}
144	
145	// counts reports whether harnessName's CostUSD should accumulate. The
146	// result is memoized; callers that want fresh resolution should
147	// construct a new tracker. Lookups for empty harness names default to
148	// true (count) so accidentally-missing harness metadata never silently
149	// bypasses the cap.
150	func (t *CostCapTracker) counts(harnessName string) bool {
151		if harnessName == "" {
152			return true
153		}
154		t.mu.Lock()
155		if v, ok := t.cache[harnessName]; ok {
156			t.mu.Unlock()
157			return v
158		}
159		t.mu.Unlock()
160		result := true
161		if t.Lookup != nil {
162			result = t.Lookup(harnessName)
163		}
164		t.mu.Lock()
165		t.cache[harnessName] = result
166		t.mu.Unlock()
167		return result
168	}
169	
170	// Add accumulates costUSD against the running total when harnessName's
171	// billing class counts toward the cap. Non-positive cost is ignored.
172	func (t *CostCapTracker) Add(harnessName string, costUSD float64) {
173		if costUSD <= 0 {
174			return
175		}
176		if !t.counts(harnessName) {
177			return
178		}
179		t.mu.Lock()
180		t.spent += costUSD
181		t.mu.Unlock()
182	}
183	
184	// Spent returns the current accumulated billed total.
185	func (t *CostCapTracker) Spent() float64 {
186		t.mu.Lock()
187		defer t.mu.Unlock()
188		return t.spent
189	}
190	
191	// Tripped reports whether the accumulated spend has reached MaxUSD. When
192	// MaxUSD <= 0 the cap is disabled and Tripped returns (_, false).
193	// Returns the formatted operator-facing detail string when tripped so
194	// callers can populate a stop-the-loop ExecuteBeadReport.
195	func (t *CostCapTracker) Tripped() (string, bool) {
196		if t.MaxUSD <= 0 {
197			return "", false
198		}
199		t.mu.Lock()
200		spent := t.spent
201		t.mu.Unlock()
202		if spent < t.MaxUSD {
203			return "", false
204		}
205		return fmt.Sprintf("cost cap reached: $%.2f billed >= $%.2f cap; raise the cap or set 0 to disable. Subscription and local providers do not count.", spent, t.MaxUSD), true
206	}
